title: 10 best herbal creams for dry eyes

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Kansas Coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds like kaempferol and quercetin, which help reduce inflammation in the eyes and alleviate dryness.
Antioxidant activity: The cream's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms.
Emollient effects: Kansas Coneflower contains emollients that help moisturize and soothe the eyes, providing relief from dryness, irritation, and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, creams helps with dry eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe and moisturize the affected area. The cream's hydrating agents work to lock in moisture, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with dry eyes. Additionally, Echinacea's antioxidant properties help protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage caused by environmental stressors and oxidative processes. This natural approach can provide relief and promote overall eye health.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and protect the eyes from oxidative stress, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms.
Alkaloids (e.g., echinacoside, cichoric acid): These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, alleviating dry eye symptoms.
Flavonoids (e.g., quercetin, kaempferol): These compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and promote overall eye health.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties beneficial for dry eye relief.
Leaves: They are used for their mild anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to calm and protect the eyes.
Flowers: They are used for their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce eye irritation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 echinacea angustifolia roots in the fall when the plants are dormant and dry them thoroughly for 2-3 weeks.
Grind 1-2 cups of dried echinacea roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Infuse 2 cups of distilled water with 2 tablespoons of echinacea powder and 1 teaspoon of beeswax in a double boiler for 10-15 minutes.
Combine 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil with the infused echinacea mixture and stir until smooth.
Pour the echinacea mixture into small glass jars and let them cool and solidify for 30-60 minutes before use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce inflammation in the eye caused by dryness, promoting healthy tear flow and reducing redness.
Hydrophilic properties: Aloe vera's hydrophilic (water-loving) molecules attract and retain moisture, providing long-lasting hydration to the eyes and helping to soothe and calm dry, irritated eyes.

Antioxidant properties: Aloe vera's antioxidants, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall eye health and reducing the risk of age-related eye diseases.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with dry eyes because of its moisturizing and soothing properties. The gel extracted from the aloe vera plant is rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as antioxidants and minerals. These nutrients help to lock in moisture and reduce inflammation in the eyes, providing relief from dryness and discomfort. By lubricating and protecting the eyes, aloe vera creams can help to alleviate dry eye symptoms and promote overall eye health. Regular use can lead to long-lasting relief.
remedy_constituents: Glucomannan: Helps with dry eyes by forming a protective barrier on the surface of the eyes, reducing water loss and providing moisture.
Vitamins (A, C, E): Contributes to the reduction of oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, promoting a healthy eye environment and alleviating dryness.
Fatty acids (especially arachidonic acid): Helps to soothe and lubricate the eyes by reducing inflammation and promoting the production of natural tears, thus alleviating dry eye symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The most used part of Aloe vera for dry eyes creams due to its high content of soothing and moisturizing compounds.
Gel from Leaves: The clear gel extracted from the leaves has anti-inflammatory and hydrating properties that help to relieve dry eyes.
Extract from Leaves: The extract from Aloe vera leaves contains vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that promote eye health and reduce dryness.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of aloe vera gel from 3-4 mature leaves and 1/4 cup of coconut oil.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler at 150°f for 10 minutes.
Combine 1/4 cup of distilled water with 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil in a small bowl.
Add the aloe vera gel mixture to the beeswax mixture in the double boiler and stir until combined.
Pour the final mixture into small containers and let it cool and set for at least 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Gamma-linolenic acid (GLA): GLA, an omega-6 fatty acid, helps reduce inflammation and promotes healing in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of dry eye disease such as redness and irritation.

Boragina: The alkaloid boragina in borage has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and inflammation.
Vitamins A and E: Borage creams often contain vitamins A and E, which are essential antioxidants that help protect the eyes' delicate tissues from oxidative stress and promote healthy vision.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borago officinalis, also known as borage, creams helps with dry eyes because of its high content of gamma linolenic acid (GLA), an omega-6 fatty acid. This essential fatty acid is crucial for maintaining the health of the tear film and protecting the eyes from dryness and irritation. By promoting the production of healthy tears, borage creams can provide relief and soothe dry, itchy eyes, making it an effective natural remedy for individuals suffering from dry eye syndrome. Regular use can lead to long-term eye health benefits.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Borago officinalis creams may help with dry eyes by reducing inflammation and improving the integrity of the tear film, which helps to protect and moisturize the eyes.

Flavonoids (specifically, kaempferol and isorhapontigenin): Flavonoids in Borago officinalis creams may help with dry eyes by exerting antioxidant effects that help to protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation.

Phytosterols (specifically, beta-sitosterol): Phytosterols in Borago officinalis creams may help with dry eyes by reducing inflammation and improving the health of the meibomian glands, which produce the lipid layer of the tear film.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm and protect dry eyes.
Seeds: Utilized for their omega-3 fatty acid content, which helps to reduce inflammation and promote healthy eye tissue.
Flowers: Employed for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in protecting and soothing dry, irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh leaves and flowers of the plant in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Blend the harvested plant material with 100ml of cold-pressed olive oil in a blender for 2 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids after 30 minutes.
Mix the strained oil with 50g of beeswax and 10ml of vitamin e oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 10-15 minutes while stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully incorporated.

remedy_properties: Emollient and moisturizing properties: Marshmallow cream soothes and lubricates the eyes, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by dryness.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness and swelling associated with dry eye syndrome, promoting a healthier ocular environment.
Antioxidant activity: Herbal marshmallow creams contain antioxidants that protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis, also known as marshmallow, creams helps with dry eyes because of its soothing and moisturizing properties. The root of the marshmallow plant contains mucilages, which are complex carbohydrates that form a protective barrier on the surface of the eyes, locking in moisture and reducing irritation. As a result, Althaea officinalis creams can help to alleviate dryness, redness, and inflammation associated with dry eyes, promoting a more comfortable and healthy ocular environment. This makes it an effective natural remedy for dry eye relief.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages in Marshmallow creams help soothe and lubricate the eyes, providing relief from dryness and irritation.
Triterpenoids and glycosides: Triterpenoids and glycosides in Marshmallow have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce redness and swelling associated with dry eyes.
Phenolic acids and flavonoids: Phenolic acids and flavonoids in Marshmallow exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and promote overall eye health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Althaea officinalis are used due to their high mucilage content, which can provide soothing and moisturizing properties to relieve dry eye symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain flavonoids and mucilages, which help to reduce inflammation and provide a protective barrier for the eyes.
Barks: The barks are used due to their high mucilage content, similar to the roots, which can help to soothe and lubricate the eyes, reducing dryness and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried roots of althaea officinalis for use in herbal cream preparation.
Infuse 200ml of water with 30g of dried roots in a saucepan over low heat for 20 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth into a bowl to separate the liquid from solids.
Combine 100g of candelilla wax and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler and heat until melted for 15 minutes.
Mix the infused liquid with 100g of coconut oil and the melted wax mixture to create a smooth cream consistency.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold contains flavonoids and carotenoids that reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from dry eye symptoms.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold help to neutralize free radicals that can damage the eyes and worsen dry eye conditions, promoting healthy ocular tissue.
Emollient and soothing properties: Pot marigold's emollients and mucilages help to moisturize and soothe the eyes, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with dry eye syndrome.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with dry eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream reduces redness and irritation, providing relief from dryness and discomfort. Its ability to calm and protect the delicate skin around the eyes also helps to lock in moisture, reducing the risk of further irritation and promoting a healthy environment for the eyes. Regular use can help to alleviate dry eye symptoms and promote overall eye comfort.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpenes: These compounds help reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms.
Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, such as oleanolic acid, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and protect the eyes, reducing dryness and irritation.
Flavonoids (specifically, tricin and quercetin): These antioxidants help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting healthy tear production and reducing dry eye symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect the eyes.
Leaves: They contain triterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that aid in healing and protecting dry eyes.
Seeds: They are rich in essential fatty acids, particularly linoleic acid, which helps to nourish and moisturize the eyes, reducing dryness and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh calendula flowers at peak bloom for optimal potency and fragrance.
Dry the flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to preserve their properties.
Steep 1/2 cup of dried calendula flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a clear liquid.
Mix 1/2 cup of the infused oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Eyebright creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation in the eyes caused by dry eye syndrome.

Antioxidants: The antioxidant properties of eyebright cream help neutralize free radicals that can damage the eyes and exacerbate dry eye symptoms, promoting overall eye health and reducing oxidative stress.
Humectant effects: Eyebright cream's humectant properties help retain moisture in the eyes, soothing and lubricating dry, irritated eyes by forming a protective barrier on the surface of the cornea.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis, also known as eyebright, creams helps with dry eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herb contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils that work together to reduce inflammation and protect the delicate tissues of the eyes. Eyebright creams can help to calm and moisturize the eyes, reducing redness and irritation associated with dry eyes. By providing a protective barrier and promoting healing, these creams can help to alleviate the discomfort of dry, irritated eyes.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: Iridoid glycosides in Euphrasia officinalis creams help to reduce inflammation and soothe the eyes, providing relief from dryness and irritation.

Phenolic acids (such as caffeic acid): Phenolic acids in Euphrasia officinalis have antioxidant properties, which help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and reduce the risk of dry eye syndrome.
Tannins: Tannins in Euphrasia officinalis have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the production of excess tears and provide relief from dry, itchy eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Euphrasia officinalis are commonly used to make creams for dry eyes because they contain bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote healthy tear production.
Flowers: The flowers of Euphrasia officinalis are used in creams for dry eyes due to their high content of glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.

Roots: The roots of Euphrasia officinalis are also used to make creams for dry eyes, as they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and promote eye health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers in late spring when in full bloom for 2 weeks.
Dry the harvested plant material at 40°c for 24 hours to remove moisture.
Combine 100g of dried euphrasia officinalis with 50g of jojoba oil and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes then allow it to cool and solidify completely.
Whip the cooled mixture with a hand mixer for 5 minutes until light and fluffy.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal licorice creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with dry eye syndrome, promoting comfort and relief.
Soother: Licorice root has natural soothing properties that can calm irritated eyes, providing instant relief from dryness, itchiness, and burning sensations.

Moisturizer: Herbal licorice creams often contain emollients and humectants that help lock in moisture, nourishing and protecting the delicate tissues of the eye and eyelid to alleviate dryness and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with dry eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active compounds, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, work together to reduce irritation and calm the eyes. As a natural demulcent, it forms a protective barrier on the ocular surface, locking in moisture and reducing evaporation. This helps to alleviate dryness, itchiness, and discomfort associated with dry eyes, making it a popular natural remedy for individuals seeking relief from this common condition.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoid saponins (specifically, Glycyrrhizin and 18α-Glycyrrhetinic acid): These compounds help to reduce inflammation and promote the integrity of the tear film, which can help alleviate dry eye symptoms.
Phenolic acids (specifically, Isoferulic acid and Ferulic acid): These antioxidants help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to dry eye disease.

Flavonoids (specifically, Licochalcone A and Glycosides): These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes, alleviating dry eye symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and moisturize dry eyes.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, reducing eye irritation and promoting healing.
Barks: Include triterpenoid saponins that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, helping to calm dry and irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots for their medicinal properties.
Mix 50 grams of the dried roots with 100 grams of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes or until it reaches 60 degrees celsius.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove solids.
Whip 100 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of shea butter into the mixture until smooth.

remedy_properties: Mucilages: Slippery elm cream contains mucilages, which are soothing, protective, and demulcent substances that help to moisturize and lubricate the eyes, reducing dryness and discomfort.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in slippery elm cream can help to neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can exacerbate dry eye symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Slippery elm cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds that may help to reduce inflammation in the eyes, which is a common underlying cause of dry eye syndrome.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ulmus rubra, also known as slippery elm, creams helps with dry eyes because of its unique properties. The bark of the Ulmus rubra tree is rich in mucilages, which are complex carbohydrates that form a soothing, protective barrier on the surface of the eyes. This barrier helps to lock in moisture, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dry eyes. Additionally, Ulmus rubra creams have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, further alleviating dry eye symptoms and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: Tannins, a type of phenolic compound, can help reduce inflammation and protect the surface of the eyes, alleviating dryness and irritation.

Flavonoids (including Quercetin): Flavonoids have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce redness and swelling associated with dry eyes, while also promoting the health of the ocular surface.

Saponins: Saponins, found in the bark of Ulmus rubra, have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which could help calm and moisturize the eyes, alleviating dryness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks of Ulmus rubra are used to make creams for dry eyes due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Ulmus rubra are used to make creams for dry eyes due to their emollient and soothing properties, which help to calm and moisturize the eyes.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Ulmus rubra are used to make creams for dry eyes due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce eye irritation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried ulmus rubra leaves and stems by cutting them at the base of the plant during peak flowering season.
Combine the harvested ulmus rubra with 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids after 10 minutes of pressing.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 10 drops of vitamin e oil to the strained mixture and heat for 10 minutes.
Pour the hot mixture into small containers and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes to an hour.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal grape creams rich in antioxidants like vitamin C, E, and beta-carotene help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the eyes, leading to dryness and irritation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in herbal grape creams, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from dry eye symptoms like itchiness and burning.

Moisturizing properties: The natural oils and humectants found in herbal grape creams help lock in moisture on the surface of the eyes, soothing and lubricating the conjunctiva and cornea to alleviate dryness and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Vitis vinifera, also known as grape, creams helps with dry eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The extract of Vitis vinifera, rich in polyphenols and flavonoids, soothes and calms the eyes, reducing irritation and redness. It also improves tear quality and increases tear production, providing long-lasting moisture and comfort to dry, irritated eyes. This natural and gentle approach helps to alleviate dry eye symptoms, promoting a healthy and comfortable eye environment. Regular use can provide significant relief.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms.
Resveratrol: This polyphenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the eyes and improve tear quality, alleviating dry eye discomfort.
Quercetin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and protect the eyes from dryness, promoting healthy tear production and reducing eye irritation.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are rich in antioxidants and fatty acids, which help to moisturize and soothe the eyes.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and tannins that have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing eye irritation and dryness.
Buds: They are a rich source of salicylic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Gather 200 grams of dried vitis vinifera flowers and leaves and store in a cool dry place for 2 days.
Crush 50 grams of the gathered vitis vinifera flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine 100 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat until melted for 20 minutes.
Mix 50 grams of the crushed vitis vinifera powder with 50 grams of distilled water and heat the mixture in a saucepan for 5 minutes.
Gradually add the beeswax and coconut oil mixture to the vitis vinifera mixture and stir until well combined for 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds help protect the eyes from oxidative stress, inflammation, and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to reduce inflammation in the eyes, which may help alleviate dry eye discomfort, itching, and redness.
Vascular protection: The plant's flavonoids and terpenoids may help improve blood flow to the eyes, reducing the risk of ischemia (reduced blood supply) and promoting healthy tear production.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with dry eyes because of its unique properties that promote eye health. The cream's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties help to reduce inflammation and repair damaged tissues in the eyes. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's flavonoids and terpenoids help to improve blood circulation and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms. By providing a protective barrier and promoting a healthy ocular environment, Ginkgo biloba creams can help to alleviate dry eye discomfort and promote overall eye well-being.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba help with dry eyes by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the eyes, thereby promoting healthy tear production and reducing oxidative stress.

Terpenoids (specifically, Ginkgolides): Ginkgolides in Ginkgo biloba help with dry eyes by blocking the action of leukotrienes, which are pro-inflammatory mediators that can exacerbate dry eye symptoms and cause tissue damage.

Bilobalide (a sesquiterpene lactone): Bilobalide in Ginkgo biloba helps with dry eyes by reducing inflammation, modulating the immune response, and improving the integrity of the tear film, ultimately promoting healthy eye function and reducing dry eye symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of the active ingredients used in creams for dry eyes due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids.
Seeds: They are used to extract bilobalide, a key compound that helps to protect and soothe the eyes.
Barks: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids that are also found in the leaves, contributing to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 ginkgo biloba leaves from a healthy plant when they are young and tender.
Steep 20-30 ginkgo biloba leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids then mix it with 1/2 cup of coconut oil.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil to the mixture.
Heat the mixture over low heat then pour it into small containers and let it cool.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with dry eyes because they contain curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory agent that reduces inflammation in the eye tissues. This inflammation is often the underlying cause of dry eye syndrome, characterized by itching, burning, and blurred vision. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the affected areas, reducing discomfort and promoting healing. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties may also help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, further supporting overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Curcumin's anti-inflammatory properties: Curcumin, a key compound in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with dry eyes.
Antioxidant properties: Turmeric cream's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals that can damage the eyes and exacerbate dryness, promoting healthy eye function.

Wound healing and tissue regeneration: The bioactive compounds in turmeric cream have been shown to stimulate wound healing and tissue regeneration, which can help soothe and repair damaged corneal tissues associated with dry eyes.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Roots: Contain a high concentration of volatile oils that can help reduce inflammation and promote healthy eye function.
remedy_recipe: Grind 2 tablespoons of dried turmeric powder into a fine paste using a mortar and pestle for 3 minutes.
Mix the turmeric paste with 2 ounces of sweet almond oil and 2 ounces of beeswax in a double boiler until fully incorporated.
Add 10 drops of tea tree essential oil and 5 drops of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir well for 2 minutes.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted, stirring occasionally.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool at room temperature for 30 minutes before transferring it into a glass jar for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea creams helps with dry eyes because they are infused with potent herbs that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties in herbal teas, such as chamomile and green tea, work to reduce redness and discomfort associated with dry eyes. As the cream is applied to the affected area, it provides a gentle and nourishing barrier that helps to moisturize and protect the delicate eye tissues, providing fast and effective relief from dry, itchy, and irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Antioxidants found in herbs like green tea, chamomile, and calendula help neutralize free radicals that can damage the eyes' natural moisture barrier, reducing inflammation and promoting healthier eye tissues.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal ingredients such as turmeric, ginger, and peppermint contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from dry eye symptoms like itchiness and irritation.

Emollients and humectants: Herbs like aloe vera, chamomile, and marshmallow root contain emollients and humectants that moisturize and soothe the eyes, locking in moisture to help alleviate dryness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, tea roots help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Barks: Containing tannins and other compounds, tea barks have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and swelling associated with dry eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh camellia sinensis leaves and rinse thoroughly with distilled water.
Steam the leaves for 30 minutes to release their bioactive compounds and essential oils.
Mix the steamed leaves with 1 cup of cold-pressed coconut oil and 1/2 cup of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 150°f for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully incorporated.
Allow the cream to cool and set at room temperature for at least 30 minutes before transferring to an air-tight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop creams helps with dry eyes because they contain soothing and nourishing properties that calm and moisturize the eyes. The maypop plant, also known as Passiflora incarnata, has been traditionally used to treat eye inflammation and irritation. When applied topically in a cream form, it provides long-lasting relief from dryness and itchiness, while also reducing redness and swelling. As a natural remedy, maypop creams work gently with the eyes to restore comfort and clarity, providing a solution for those suffering from chronic dry eye conditions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Maypop's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as saponins and flavonoids, help reduce inflammation in the eyes, which is a common underlying cause of dry eye syndrome.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Maypop cream, like vitamin C and beta-carotene, protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate dry eye symptoms.

Soothing and lubricating properties: The emollient and soothing compounds in Maypop cream, such as saponins and glycosides, help to calm and moisturize the eyes, providing instant relief for dry, irritated eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Fruits: Contain fatty acids and vitamins that moisturize and nourish the eyes, relieving dryness and itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 250 grams of fresh passionflower leaves and stems to ensure potency.
Combine dried passionflower material with 100 grams of beeswax and 200 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a heat-proof mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the wax is fully incorporated and melted.
Strain the hot cream through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove solids and ensure smooth texture.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify at room temperature for at least 2 hours before transferring to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort creams helps with dry eyes because they contain potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The cream's rich, moisturizing properties help to lock in moisture and protect the ocular surface, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dry eyes. Additionally, Asiatic pennywort has been shown to promote healthy tear production and improve the overall health of the cornea, providing long-lasting relief from dry, itchy, and irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Asiatic pennywort cream contains antioxidants that help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to dry eye syndrome.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Asiatic pennywort cream help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with dry eyes, promoting a soothing and calming effect on the ocular surface.
Moisturizing property: Asiatic pennywort cream contains emollients that help lock in moisture and soothe dry, irritated eyes, providing long-lasting relief from dry eye discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in soothing and calming dry eyes.
Rhyzomes: Containing mucilages and saponins, which help to lubricate and protect the eyes from irritation.
Barks: High in flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve ocular blood flow.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 50g of centella asiatica powder and mix it with 100ml of distilled water in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the powder is fully dissolved and a smooth paste forms.
Add 20% glycerin by weight to the mixture and stir well, then let it cool down to room temperature for 30 minutes.
Combine 10g of beeswax and 5g of coconut oil in a small saucepan, heat over low heat until melted and smooth, then remove from heat.
Mix the cooled herbal extract with the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture, stirring well to combine, and pour into sterilized containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn creams helps with dry eyes because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the affected area. The extracts from the hawthorn plant, such as flavonoids and anthocyanins, work to reduce redness and irritation, providing fast relief from dry, itchy, and gritty eyes. Additionally, hawthorn's emollient properties help to lock in moisture, hydrating the eyes and surrounding skin for a more comfortable and refreshed appearance. This natural remedy effectively addresses the root causes of dry eye syndrome, offering long-lasting comfort and protection.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Hawthorn creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), which help reduce inflammation in the eyes and alleviate dryness, irritation, and redness.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in hawthorn creams, such as vitamin C and E, help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eye syndrome.
Emollient and moisturizing agents: Hawthorn creams contain emollients like fatty acids and glycerin, which help lock in moisture and soothe the eyes, reducing dryness, itchiness, and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, hawthorn roots help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy vision.
Seeds: Hawthorn seeds contain omega-3 fatty acids and vitamin E, which work together to moisturize and nourish the eyes, providing long-lasting relief from dry eye symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh crataegus monogyna leaves and stems for infusion.
Combine the harvested material with 500ml of boiling distilled water and let it steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container to discard solids.
Add 20g of beeswax, 10g of coconut oil, and 5g of olive oil to the liquid extract, then heat it gently until the mixture reaches a temperature of 60°c for 15 minutes.
Pour the hot mixture into a sterilized cream jar and let it cool to room temperature before sealing and storing in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow gentian creams helps with dry eyes because they contain a combination of herbs that work together to soothe and moisturize the eyes. The yellow gentian extract has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and irritation, while the other herbal ingredients provide long-lasting lubrication and protection for the ocular surface. This unique blend creates a nourishing environment that helps to calm and replenish the delicate tissues of the eyes, providing relief from dryness, itchiness, and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yellow gentian contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with dry eye syndrome, promoting healthy eyes and reducing redness.
Antioxidant: Gentian's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage the eyes and worsen dry eye symptoms, protecting the eyes from further irritation and inflammation.

Sothing and demulcent: The cream's soothing and demulcent properties provide a protective barrier on the surface of the eye, calming irritation, and relieving dryness, itchiness, and burning sensations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are extracted to provide soothing and anti-inflammatory properties for dry eye relief.
Buds: The immature buds contain high concentrations of flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry gentiana lutea roots, processing them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the gentiana lutea powder with beeswax, coconut oil, and distilled water in a double boiler, melting the mixture until smooth.
Add glycerin and panthenol to the mixture, stirring well to combine, before heating it to 60°c for 10 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat, let it cool to room temperature, then whip it with an electric mixer until light and fluffy, incorporating aloe vera gel at this stage.
Pour the whipped cream into sterilized containers, seal them tightly, and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams help with dry eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the eyes. The herb contains hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, providing relief from dryness and irritation. Additionally, St John's Wort has natural antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections that can contribute to dry eye syndrome, promoting healthy tear production and overall ocular health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: St John's Wort cream may help reduce inflammation and alleviate dry, irritated eyes by decreasing the production of pro-inflammatory mediators.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant compounds in St John's Wort cream can neutralize free radicals that damage the eyes, potentially reducing oxidative stress and promoting healthy vision.
Immunomodulatory effects: St John's Wort cream may modulate the immune response to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes, which can be beneficial for dry eye syndrome patients.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots are extracted and processed into creams to soothe and calm dry, irritated eyes.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and hyperforin, the flowers are used to create anti-inflammatory and antioxidant-rich creams that protect and moisturize dry eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort flowers and leaves from a trusted source ensuring purity quality.
Dry the harvested plant material using shade drying or air circulation for 7-10 days duration.
Combine dried hypericum perforatum with sweet almond oil and beeswax in a 5:3:1 ratio by weight proportion.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler or thermometer-controlled heating source at 160°f to 180°f temperature range.
Allow the cooled mixture to thicken and set for 24-48 hours before transferring it into sterile containers storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with dry eyes because they provide a soothing, natural relief for irritated and inflamed eye tissues. The anti-inflammatory properties of fennel work to reduce redness and discomfort, while its moisturizing effects help to replenish the eye's natural tear layer. As a result, fennel creams can effectively alleviate dry eye symptoms such as itchiness, burning, and blurred vision, providing long-lasting comfort and relief for those suffering from this common condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fennel contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which help to reduce inflammation in the eyes and alleviate symptoms of dry eye disease.
Moisturizing properties: Fennel's emollient and humectant properties help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, irritated eyes, providing temporary relief from discomfort and dryness.

Antioxidant activity: Fennel's antioxidant compounds help to protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eye disease and other ocular disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming dry, irritated eyes.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve moisture levels in the eye, relieving dryness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves in the morning after dew has dried off.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours.
Combine 20 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 48 hours to infuse the oil with herbal properties.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth and store it in an amber glass bottle for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with dry eyes because they provide a soothing, natural relief for irritated eyes. The dandelion root extract in these creams has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling caused by dry eye syndrome. Additionally, the moisturizing ingredients in dandelion creams help to lock in moisture on the surface of the eye, providing long-lasting comfort and relief from the dry, itchy sensation associated with this condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms.

Soil-removing and demulcent properties: Dandelion's ability to remove impurities from the skin and create a protective barrier helps soothe and lubricate the eyes, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with dry eye.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Dandelion's antimicrobial compounds help combat infections that can contribute to chronic dry eye conditions, such as blepharitis and conjunctivitis, by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria and fungi.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, dandelion roots are used to soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Fruits: The fruits of the dandelion plant contain vitamins A and C, which help to nourish and protect the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves in the morning for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 150°f for 24 hours.
Combine 100 grams of dried taraxacum officinale leaves with 500 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture slowly over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully dissolved and the mixture reaches a temperature of 180°f after 30 minutes of preparation time.
Strain the cream through cheesecloth into clean glass jars, discarding the solids, and allow it to cool before refrigerating for at least 2 hours before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for dry eyes are topical ointments infused with natural herbs and extracts that provide relief from dry, irritated eyes. They work by moisturizing the eyes and soothing inflammation. Examples include creams containing aloe vera, chamomile, and green tea, which can help reduce symptoms of dry eye syndrome. By providing long-lasting hydration, these creams can improve lives by allowing individuals to watch TV, use digital devices, and engage in daily activities without discomfort.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry eyes that are treated with herbal creams are skin conditions such as eczema, psoriasis, and rosacea. These conditions often manifest with symptoms like itchiness, redness, and flakiness, which can be alleviated by the anti-inflammatory and soothing properties of herbal creams. Additionally, acne, dermatitis, and contact dermatitis may also be treated with herbal creams to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with dry eyes is a blend of Aloe Vera, Green Tea, and Eyebright. Aloe Vera soothes and moisturizes the eyes, while Green Tea reduces inflammation and promotes healing. Eyebright, rich in antioxidants, helps to protect the eyes from damage and promotes healthy tear production. Together, these herbal creams create a powerful synergy that can help to alleviate dry eye symptoms, reduce redness, and promote overall eye health and comfort. Regular application can lead to long-term relief.
